followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sochna ki class methods RFC-enabled ho sakti hain agar REMOTE attribute set karo — yeh option exists nahi karta. Function module ka RFC attribute hota hai; class methods ka nahi.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" RFC-enabled function module (SE37, RFC attribute = Remote Module)
FUNCTION z_get_customer_data.
*  RFC-enabled: any external system can call this
*  IMPORTING iv_kunnr   TYPE kunnr
*  EXPORTING es_customer TYPE kna1
*  EXCEPTIONS not_found = 1.
  SELECT SINGLE * INTO es_customer FROM kna1
    WHERE kunnr = iv_kunnr.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    RAISE not_found.
  ENDIF.
ENDFUNCTION.
" Class method: cannot be made RFC-enabled
" Modern alternative: OData service backed by class method

followupAnswerBank.question: What is a function group and how does it differ from a class?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Function group (SE80 mein, transaction SE37 se access) ek container hai multiple function modules ke liye. Global data jo function group mein declare ho — sab function modules share karte hain. Jaise ek department ke sab employees ek shared office use karte hain. Class: ek self-contained unit with attributes (data) aur methods, encapsulated — PRIVATE/PUBLIC/PROTECTED control. Function group global data — anyone in the group reads and writes. Class data — encapsulated, controlled access via methods. Class zyada structured aur safe hai — class preferred hoti hai naye code mein.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A function group is a container for related function modules and their shared global data. The global data is accessible to all function modules in the group — think of it as shared state within a namespace. A class is fundamentally different: it encapsulates data with controlled access levels — PRIVATE attributes cannot be accessed outside the class, only through PUBLIC methods. A function group has no encapsulation enforcement — any code knowing the global data name could potentially access it. A class provides true information hiding. Additionally, a class can be instantiated multiple times with independent state; a function group has one shared global state per session.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Function group global data ka side effect: ek function module mein data set karo, doosra function module woh data read karta hai — implicit coupling. Ye debugging nightmare hai. Class attributes explicit control se aisa coupling prevent karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Function group ko class ki tarah treat karna — they are architecturally different. Function group is namespace + shared state. Class is encapsulated object with defined interface. Do not mix the mental models.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Function Group global data (shared, uncontrolled)
" FUNCTION-POOL zfg_tax.  " Function group header
DATA: gv_tax_rate TYPE p DECIMALS 2,  " Shared by ALL function modules
      gv_country  TYPE land1.           " Anyone can access/modify

" Class: encapsulated (controlled)
CLASS zcl_tax_config DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS get_rate RETURNING VALUE(rv_rate) TYPE p.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    DATA mv_rate    TYPE p DECIMALS 2.  " Only accessible via method
    DATA mv_country TYPE land1.         " Not directly accessible
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you migrate a FORM subroutine to a class method in modern ABAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Migration steps: 1) Existing FORM identify karo — parameters list karo (USING/CHANGING). 2) New class banao ya existing class mein method add karo with same parameters — IMPORTING/RETURNING ya EXPORTING. 3) FORM ka code copy karo method mein — cleanup karo: global data access ko parameter se replace karo, SY-SUBRC checks ko exception classes se replace karo. 4) PERFORM calls ko method calls se replace karo. 5) Unit tests likho naye method ke liye. 6) FORM ko DEPRECATED comment mark karo — remove karo jab callers migrate ho jaye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Migration process: identify the FORM with its USING and CHANGING parameters. Create a class method with equivalent IMPORTING, RETURNING, or CHANGING parameters — prefer RETURNING over CHANGING for clean functional style. Copy the FORM logic into the method, replacing global data access with injected dependencies and sy-subrc patterns with exception classes. Replace PERFORM calls with method calls at all call sites. Write unit tests for the new method before removing the FORM. Mark the FORM as deprecated or delete it once all callers are migrated. This is incremental — migrate one FORM at a time, not all at once.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ek common challenge: FORM global data use karta hai — gv_* variables. Method mein inka equivalent inject karna padta hai via constructor or parameter. Yeh migration good opportunity hai hidden global state dependencies expose karne ki.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: FORM se method mein sirf code copy karna bina parameter refactoring ke — phir method bhi global variables use karti hai. True migration means replacing global data access with explicit parameters or injected dependencies.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Before: FORM with global data access
DATA gv_rate TYPE p DECIMALS 2.  " Global
FORM calc_tax USING iv_amount  TYPE netwr
              CHANGING cv_tax   TYPE netwr.
  cv_tax = iv_amount * gv_rate.  " Global dependency!
ENDFORM.

" After: Method with explicit parameters
CLASS zcl_tax_calc DEFINITION PUBLIC FINAL.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS calculate
      IMPORTING iv_amount   TYPE netwr
                iv_rate     TYPE p  " Explicit - no global
      RETURNING VALUE(rv_tax) TYPE netwr.
ENDCLASS.
CLASS zcl_tax_calc IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD calculate.
    rv_tax = iv_amount * iv_rate.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

followupAnswerBank.question: When should you still use function modules in modern SAP development?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Function module use karo jab technically required ho — no OO alternative exists: 1) RFC: bahar se SAP call karni hai — function module RFC-enabled karna padta hai. 2) BAPIs: standard SAP-delivered function modules hain — use karo as-is, modify nahi karo. 3) SmartForms: driver program ek function module hota hai — SmartForms ne yahi architecture expect ki hai. 4) Older enhancement frameworks: kernel exits, some classic enhancement spots function modules expect karte hain. 5) Legacy integration: existing RFC-based middleware (PI/PO integrations) change karna expensive aur risky hai — existing function modules maintain karo. In cases ke bahar: new Z development mein class methods prefer karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Function modules remain the required choice in four specific scenarios: RFC-enabled interfaces where external systems call SAP — only function modules support RFC attributes; standard SAP BAPIs which are function modules by design and should be used as delivered; SmartForms which require a function module driver program as the architectural entry point; and legacy enhancement spots like kernel exits that expect function module implementations. For all new custom business logic development, class methods in OO classes are the recommended and Clean ABAP-compliant approach. I never write new function modules for logic that could live in a class method.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Project pe: existing RFC-based interfaces ka inventory banao. Ye function modules maintain karne honge long-term. New interfaces ke liye: REST/OData via Gateway — inke peeche class methods aur CDS views hote hain. Gradual shift — old RF Cs maintain, new integrations modern architecture mein.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: New business logic ke liye function module banana kyunki 'team FORM aur FM comfortable hai.' Comfort zone is not a technical justification — class methods are the standard and team capability should be developed.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Valid function module: RFC interface
FUNCTION z_rfc_get_material_data.
*  RFC attribute: Remote-enabled module
*  Called by: external system via RFC
*  No OO alternative for RFC!
  SELECT SINGLE * INTO es_material FROM mara
    WHERE matnr = iv_matnr.
ENDFUNCTION.

" Valid: BAPI (standard, use as delivered)
CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2'
  EXPORTING order_header_in = ls_header
  IMPORTING salesdocument   = lv_vbeln.

" New logic: use class method (not FM)
DATA(lv_result) = lo_calc->process( lv_vbeln ).

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the exception handling difference between function modules and methods?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Function module exceptions: EXCEPTIONS clause mein named exceptions define karo (my_error = 1, not_found = 2). Caller ke paas CALL FUNCTION ke baad SY-SUBRC check karo — 0 matlab success, 1 matlab my_error, 2 matlab not_found. Problem: compiler force nahi karta ki caller handle kare — bhool gaye check karna? Silent failure. Method exceptions: RAISING clause mein exception class names do (RAISING zcx_not_found). TRY-CATCH block use karo caller mein. CX_STATIC_CHECK se inherit karo toh compiler force karta hai caller ko handle ya propagate karna. Much safer — compile-time guarantee.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Function module exception handling uses the EXCEPTIONS clause with integer codes and SY-SUBRC after the call — if the caller forgets to check SY-SUBRC, the error is silently swallowed. There is no compile-time enforcement. Class method exception handling uses the RAISING clause with exception class types. For CX_STATIC_CHECK subclasses, the compiler enforces that callers either catch the exception or propagate it via their own RAISING clause. This provides compile-time safety, meaningful exception objects with context attributes, and structured TRY-CATCH-FINALLY blocks. Methods offer significantly superior error handling compared to function modules.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Legacy code audit: FM calls ke baad SY-SUBRC check hota hai kya? Often nahi hota — silent error swallowing. Ek common source of production bugs. Refactoring FM to method forces proper exception handling — immediate quality improvement.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Function module mein EXCEPTIONS use nahi karna — then caller has no way to detect errors. Always define meaningful EXCEPTIONS in function modules even if they look like old-style code. And always check SY-SUBRC in every CALL FUNCTION.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" FM exception: SY-SUBRC check (easy to forget)
CALL FUNCTION 'Z_GET_ORDER'
  IMPORTING iv_vbeln   = lv_vbeln
  EXPORTING es_order   = ls_order
  EXCEPTIONS not_found = 1
             others    = 2.
IF sy-subrc = 1.  " Developer might forget this check!
  " Handle not found
ENDIF.

" Method exception: compiler enforces handling
TRY.
  DATA(ls_order) = lo_repo->get_order( lv_vbeln ).
CATCH zcx_not_found INTO DATA(lx_err).
  " Compiler forces this catch - cannot forget
  MESSAGE lx_err->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
ENDTRY.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does CALL FUNCTION differ from calling a class method?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CALL FUNCTION: string mein function module name pass karo — runtime pe resolve hota hai. Dynamic kiya ja sakta hai (CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name). Overhead: runtime lookup, parameter marshaling. Performance thoda slower than direct method call. Direct method call (lo_obj->method): compile-time resolved, faster. Type-safe — compiler parameters check karta hai. Method reference via interface: polymorphism possible — alag implementations runtime pe swap ho sakti hain. CALL FUNCTION mein polymorphism nahi — name string hota hai, fixed at runtime.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CALL FUNCTION passes the function module name as a string — it is resolved at runtime. This allows dynamic calls (variable FM name) but means no compile-time parameter type checking beyond what the function module definition provides, and slightly more overhead due to runtime resolution and parameter marshaling. Calling a class method is resolved at compile time — the compiler verifies parameter types, counts, and names. Method calls are faster, type-safe, and support polymorphism through interface references. CALL FUNCTION syntax is also more verbose and error-prone than the clean method call syntax in modern ABAP.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Dynamic CALL FUNCTION (variable FM name) use case: plugin architecture without class instances. But this can be replaced with class factory pattern that selects and instantiates the correct class — same dynamism, better type safety and testability.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: CALL FUNCTION mein hardcoded string FM name typo — runtime error, not compile error. Method call mein typo = compile error, immediate fix. Another reason methods are safer.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" CALL FUNCTION: runtime resolved, verbose
CALL FUNCTION 'Z_CALCULATE_TAX'
  IMPORTING iv_amount  = lv_amount
            iv_country = lv_country
  EXPORTING ev_tax     = lv_tax
  EXCEPTIONS invalid   = 1.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.  " Easy to forget
ENDIF.

" Method call: compile-time resolved, concise, type-safe
TRY.
  DATA(lv_tax) = lo_calc->calculate(
    iv_amount  = lv_amount
    iv_country = lv_country ).
CATCH zcx_invalid_amount.
ENDTRY.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you explain to a team why PERFORM should not be used in new code?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: PERFORM na use karne ke teen strong reasons: 1) Testability: FORM routines ko unit test banana almost impossible hai — global data dependency, no interface to mock. 2) Scope: FORM sirf ek program mein visible hai — reuse karna chahte ho toh include file use karna padta hai — messy dependency. 3) SAP official position: Clean ABAP guide aur S/4HANA development guidelines explicitly kehte hain FORM routines avoid karo. 'SAP ne khud kaha hai' — team ko usually yeh argument convince karta hai fastest.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Three reasons I present to the team: First, testability — FORM routines typically access global data and cannot be unit tested independently; methods with constructor injection and interfaces are fully testable. Second, reusability — FORM routines are program-local; a class method can be called from anywhere once the class is instantiated, with no include file dependency management. Third, official SAP guidance — the Clean ABAP style guide explicitly states FORM routines should not be written in new code, and ATC can be configured to flag FORM usage as a finding. I also point out that the effort to write a method instead of a FORM is identical — it is purely a structural choice with significantly better long-term characteristics.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Team buy-in strategy: ATC mein FORM routine usage ko warning level finding configure karo — developers will see it in their daily ATC checks and naturally move toward methods. Top-down mandate rarely works; tool-based feedback works better.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Team ko force karna bina reason bataye — 'PERFORM mat likho, boss ne bola hai.' Always explain technical reasons aur SAP's official position. Understand first, then adopt.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Why PERFORM is problematic
" Untestable: accesses global data
DATA gv_rate TYPE p DECIMALS 2.  " Global
FORM calc USING iv_amt TYPE netwr CHANGING cv_tax TYPE netwr.
  cv_tax = iv_amt * gv_rate.  " Hidden global dependency!
ENDFORM.  " Cannot unit test this properly

" Method: testable, explicit, no globals
METHODS calculate
  IMPORTING iv_amount TYPE netwr
            iv_rate   TYPE p
  RETURNING VALUE(rv_tax) TYPE netwr.
" Unit test injects exact iv_rate value - deterministic

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the role of function modules in BAPIs and how do you use them correctly?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: BAPI (Business Application Programming Interface) SAP ke standard function modules hain jo external systems ke liye stable interface provide karte hain. Key points: 1) BAPIs naming convention: BAPI_ prefix, then object name. SE37 mein search karo. 2) Commit handling: BAPI ke baad BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT explicitly call karna padta hai — BAPI internal COMMIT nahi karta. 3) Return table: BAPIRETURN type table check karo — errors aur warnings yahan aate hain. 4) Modification: SAP-delivered BAPIs modify mat karo — enhancement framework use karo (BAdI). 5) Custom BAPI jaisi FM: RFC-enabled, BAPIRETURN table use karo, commit handle karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: BAPIs are SAP-standard RFC-enabled function modules following strict conventions. Key usage rules: always check the RETURN table for errors and warnings — BAPI does not raise classic exceptions, it populates this table. Always call BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT explicitly after successful processing — BAPIs do not auto-commit. Never modify SAP-delivered BAPI code — use BAdI implementations for custom logic extensions. For creating custom BAPI-style interfaces, follow the same conventions: RFC-enabled function module, RETURN table for messages, explicit commit handling, and comprehensive error entries in the RETURN table with message class and number for proper translation.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT bhoolna ek very common mistake hai — data DB mein nahi jaata, no error message, data silently lost. Always in a try-finally or explicit commit call after last BAPI in a logical unit of work.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: BAPI call ke baad BAPIRETURN check nahi karna — silent failures. BAPI may return TYPE = 'E' (error) in return table while SY-SUBRC = 0. Always loop through RETURN and check for TYPE = 'A' (abort) or 'E' (error).
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Correct BAPI usage pattern
DATA lt_return TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2'
  EXPORTING order_header_in = ls_header
  IMPORTING salesdocument   = lv_vbeln
  TABLES    return          = lt_return.

" Check return table - mandatory!
IF line_exists( lt_return[ type = 'E' ] ) OR
   line_exists( lt_return[ type = 'A' ] ).
  " Handle error - rollback
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_ROLLBACK'.
ELSE.
  " Success - explicit commit required!
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING wait = abap_true.
ENDIF.
